markdown:
## Mapped Relationships


1. **One-to-One (1:1):** Each record in Table A is **linked to** **only one record** in Table B, and vice versa.
2. **One-to-Many (1:M):** A record in Table A can be **linked to multiple records** in Table B, but each record in Table B is **linked to only one record** in Table A.
3. **Many-to-Many (M:N):** Records in Table A can be** linked to multiple records** in Table B, and **vice versa**.

<callout type="example">* **One-to-one relationship:**
  * A person has one passport, a passport belongs to one person.
  * **Tables:** Person and Passport

| PersonID | Name | PassportID |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Alice | 101 |
| 2 | Bob | 102 |

| PassportID | IssueDate |
| --- | --- |
| 101 | 2020-01-01 |
| 102 | 2021-02-15 |
|  |  |

* **One-to-Many relationship:**
  * A teacher can have many students, but each student has only one teacher.
  * **Tables:** Teacher and Student

| TeacherID | Name |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Mr. Smith |
| 2 | Ms. Johnson |

| StudentID | Name | TeacherID |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 101 | Alice | 1 |
| 102 | Bob | 1 |
| 103 | Charlie | 2 |
|  |  |  |



* **Many-to-many relationship:**
  * Students **enroll** in **multiple** courses, and courses have **multiple** students.
  * **Tables:** Student, Course, and Enrollment (junction table)

| StudentID | Name |
| --- | --- |
| 101 | Alice |
| 102 | Bob |

| CourseID | Title |
| --- | --- |
| 201 | Math |
| 202 | Science |

| StudentID | CourseID |
| --- | --- |
| 101 | 201 |
| 101 | 202 |
| 102 | 201 |
|  |  |

###</callout>

## How Relationships are Represented


1. **Foreign Keys:** A foreign key in one table **references** the primary key in another, **establishing** a **link** between records.
2. **Junction Tables:** In many-to-many relationships, a junction table is used to **break the relationship **into two one-to-many relationships.
